title: Pyrus hummelii
intro: Pyrus hummelii, also known as the Himalayan pear or wild pear, is a deciduous tree native to the Himalayan region and parts of Central Asia. The health benefits of Pyrus hummelii include its traditional use as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant agent, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Pyrus hummelii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Pyrus hummelii include teas, decoctions, and infusions, as well as dried extracts used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Pyrus hummelii is classified within the family Rosaceae. This species belongs to the genus Pyrus, which includes other pear trees. The classification also places Pyrus hummelii within the order Rosales. Further, it is a species of flowering plant. Pyrus hummelii is native to the Himalayan region.
